content.claim: In the smallest settlements, school viability does not decline gradually but can reach a zero-enrollment cliff. Garab (44 residents) closed its primary school because no children remained; the last birth in that village occurred nine years prior to reporting.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Single case study; does not establish systematic threshold below which closure becomes inevitable
No data on whether Garab residents with school-age children migrated out or were never present to begin with
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
